outcome.note: Measured after training the edited schema in the next iteration.

outcome_decision.reason: Hack risk improved, but internal task/semantic evidence dropped enough that this schema should not be the next base.

lesson: Shaping-goal mismatch: agent hovers near pad due to dense approach reward, unable to achieve terminal landing event. Previous rebalancing edits insufficient. Structural search needed to add a bridging reward to guide descent.

reports.reason: Metric component r_landing_region_progress has variation=0.834329, active_rate=0.442744 on current rollouts.

reflection_strategy.why_atomic_or_separable: Structural search may involve adding a new reward component and optionally adjusting existing weights; these can be applied sequentially with evaluation between steps.
editor_constraints: Any new component must have weight ≤ 0.5 to avoid becoming a new dominant dense reward
New component should be a simple descent indicator (e.g., negative altitude or vertical speed toward pad) to bridge hovering to landing
Do not remove r_approach_region entirely; it still provides essential early approach signal
Maintain one-time flags on all terminal bonuses
Total shaping reward per episode should stay within [1, 300]
Do not increase r_energy penalty beyond -0.05
must_preserve: r_approach_progress (delta positive only) for directional approach signal
r_stability to penalize wobble
r_landing_quality for future landing smoothness
r_safe_contact_once and r_stable_landing_once as one-time terminal bonuses
must_avoid: Adding dense shaping components that overshadow the terminal goal (e.g., a large altitude-based reward that recreates hovering)
Making terminal bonuses non-one-time
Reducing all shaping components to near zero without a reliable terminal gradient
Increasing r_approach_region weight
reflection_strategy.expected_effect: After retraining, the agent should begin to descend toward the pad instead of hovering. Initial mean reward may drop, but eventually terminal event will become reachable, leading to first stable landing and a rise in success rate.
reflection_strategy.risk_analysis: If the new bridging reward is still insufficient, reward collapse may continue. If the new component is too high, the agent may just bounce without stable landing. Monitoring after retraining is essential; if no terminal payment after 2 iterations, consider further relaxing terminal condition definition.

reflection_report.reflection_summary: Agent never lands: 0% success rate, terminal events never paid, unstable bouncing occurs. r_approach_region still dominates despite two coupled edits (decrease + duration relax, decrease + terminal weight boost). The failure is a shaping/goal mismatch leading to a task failure, not reward hacking. Previous edits were too weak to shift behavior. Memory suggests structural search to add a bridging reward (e.g., soft descent) to connect current hovering to the terminal event.

observed_facts: previous edits (decrease approach + relax duration, decrease approach + increase terminal weight) produced no terminal payments and minimal semantic improvement
shaping_goal_mismatch flag true, repeated_event_exploitation due to bouncing, not hacking
likely_true_failures: Agent does not land; task unsolved
Shaping rewards (especially r_approach_region) incentivize proximity without terminal completion
Policy stuck in local optimum of hovering/bouncing near pad
likely_false_positives: repeated_event_exploitation is a consequence of bouncing, not deliberate reward hacking
high_reward_low_progress flag false; component_dominance raw flag false
root_cause_hypotheses: Terminal event condition (stable_landing) still too strict/difficult for current policy to achieve even with duration reduced to 1 step; bonus weight increase insufficient
Lack of intermediate gradient from hovering to stable landing leaves agent without guidance between current behavior and terminal success
Residual r_approach_region weight (0.9 -> 0.54) still high enough to make hovering locally optimal

reusable_lessons: Coupled rebalancing (shaping + terminal) can shift focus but must be strong enough
One-time event bonuses protect against exploitation if they are triggerable
failed_or_weak_lessons: iter_000_to_001 (decrease r_approach_region 0.6 + relax duration to 1) too weak; no terminal payment
iter_002_to_003 (decrease r_approach_region 0.6 + increase r_stable_landing_once 2x) caused small score drop, no terminal payment
avoid_actions: Solo decrease of dominant shaping component without strong terminal boost
Increasing terminal bonus weight without verifying the event is reachable
Applying the exact same rebalancing package again
recommended_actions: Add a bridging reward (e.g., soft descent, negative altitude) to connect hovering to terminal landing
Consider further minor decrease of r_approach_region only if paired with a reliable new gradient
Ensure terminal condition is achievable with current policy; possibly inspect stable_landing_condition definition if edit tools allow

structural_analysis.missing_signal_hypothesis: The agent never receives a terminal reward because the required landing events (safe_contact, stable_landing) are never triggered. All dense shaping rewards (approach_region, stability, landing_quality) allow the agent to accumulate high total reward without ever landing. A progressive, intermediate signal that explicitly moves the agent closer to the landing pad is needed to bridge the gap between hovering near the pad and actually touching down.
structural_analysis.why_local_edit_is_insufficient: Previous local edits (decreasing r_approach_region weight, increasing r_stable_landing_once weight, relaxing landing duration) all failed to induce a single terminal payment. The terminal events remain unreachably sparse; only a new process signal focused on the tighter landing region can provide refined guidance.
memory_constraints: Do not repeat weight adjustments that have already failed
Do not add unprotected repeatable event bonuses
safety_constraints: Use only available task metrics and operators
Ensure the new component does not create a dominant shaping loop

reward_editor.rationale: Add a metric_delta component on 'landing_region_score' (the tighter pad-region metric) to reward incremental movement toward the landing pad. This provides a dense, intermediate process signal that complements existing shaping without creating exploit risks. It is a generic, non-environment-specific signal and uses only available metrics. The component uses positive_only=true to avoid penalizing temporary regressions, and weight is moderate to avoid overshadowing terminal incentives.

structural_response.diagnosis: Terminal landing event never triggered despite terminal reward rebalancing. Adding a metric_delta on landing_region_score creates a bridge signal to encourage approach to the precise landing zone.

Memory suggests structural search to add a bridging reward (e.g., soft descent) to connect current hovering to the terminal event.

lesson: Shaping-goal mismatch: r_approach_region dominates, terminal events never trigger. Coupled rebalancing with reduced approach weight and relaxed stable landing duration aims to shift focus to landing.

reflection_strategy.why_atomic_or_separable: Reducing approach region reward alone could drastically lower total reward and slow learning, while increasing terminal reward alone may remain unused. A balanced package ensures the agent still receives guidance but shifts focus toward landing.
editor_constraints: Do not remove r_approach_region entirely; it provides useful guidance
Do not increase total shaping reward without a corresponding increase in terminal reward signal
Consider relaxing duration conditions for terminal events to make them more achievable initially
Maintain the one-time nature of terminal bonuses but ensure they can be triggered with a single stable landing per episode
Keep r_energy penalty low to not deter main engine use
must_preserve: r_approach_progress (delta positive only) for encouraging approach
r_stability to penalize wobble
r_landing_quality to grade landing smoothness
must_avoid: Increasing r_approach_region weight
Adding new dense shaping components that could overshadow terminal goal
Making terminal event bonuses non-one-time (to avoid exploitation)
reflection_strategy.expected_effect: Expected to decrease mean reward and shift agent behavior from hovering to attempting landing. Success rate should increase after rebalancing and retraining.
reflection_strategy.risk_analysis: If terminal bonuses are still too hard to achieve, the agent might fail to get any reward and the policy could collapse. If approach reward is reduced too much, learning may stall. Careful tuning required.

reflection_report.reflection_summary: The agent gets high reward from r_approach_region but never triggers terminal landing events. All episodes reach max steps without landing, exhibiting bouncing (unstable contact). This indicates a shaping-goal mismatch: the agent is content to hover near the pad rather than land. The terminal event bonuses are one-time and never applied, so the agent lacks a strong landing signal. The root cause is likely excessive reward for approach region and insufficient terminal/completion incentive.

likely_true_failures: The agent fails to land; task not solved
Shaping rewards incentivize proximity without terminal completion
likely_false_positives: repeated_event_exploitation is likely a symptom of bouncing, not deliberate reward hacking
high_reward_low_progress flag is false, indicating it's not simple dominance hacking
root_cause_hypotheses: The r_approach_region weight (1.5) is too high relative to terminal reward, leading to a local optimum of hovering near pad
The terminal event bonuses conditions (safe_contact 2 steps, stable_landing 5 steps) may be too strict, never providing any signal

reward_editor.rationale: The agent gets high reward from r_approach_region but never triggers terminal landing events. All episodes reach max steps without landing, exhibiting bouncing. This indicates a shaping-goal mismatch: excessive approach reward and terminal events that are too strict to ever trigger. A coupled package reduces approach weight to lower its dominance while relaxing the stable landing duration to 1 step to provide a feasible terminal signal. This rebalancing should shift behavior toward landing completion without collapsing learning.
reward_editor.expected_effect: Decrease in mean episode reward initially; agent begins to attempt landing and triggers stable landing bonus; success rate rises as lander prioritizes terminal completion over hovering.
reward_editor.risk_analysis: If even with relaxed duration the agent still cannot land, total reward may drop sharply because approach region now gives less. However, r_approach_progress and other shaping components still provide directional guidance. The change is safe as long as the terminal bonus remains achievable.

what_worked: Coupled rebalancing of shaping and terminal rewards can break local optima
avoid_next: Solo decrease_weight on dominant shaping component without terminal boost
recommend_next: Consider further tuning if agent still hovers after retraining

lesson: r_approach_region still dominates, terminal never paid; coupled rebalancing: further reduce approach weight by 0.6 and double stable landing bonus to redirect toward landing.

warnings: Low step evidence for target r_stable_landing_once: ratio=0.0000, trigger_rate=0.0000; warning only.

reflection_strategy.why_atomic_or_separable: Reducing r_approach_region weight significantly could collapse total reward before the agent learns to trigger the terminal event; therefore, a simultaneous strong increase in terminal bonus weight is needed to maintain a viable learning signal. Without the coupled package, the agent may lose all dense guidance and fail to improve.
editor_constraints: Do not remove r_approach_region entirely; it provides useful early guidance to approach the pad
The total shaping reward per episode should remain within [0, 300] to prevent reward scale explosion
Terminal event bonuses must remain one-time per episode to avoid exploitation
If relaxing a duration condition further is possible, ensure it does not make the event trivially exploitable (e.g., a single frame of contact without stability)
Do not increase r_energy penalty beyond -0.05 per step to avoid discouraging main engine use
Any new shaping component (if added) must not dominate the combined terminal incentives
must_preserve: r_approach_progress (delta positive only) to maintain a directional signal toward the pad
r_stability to penalize excessive wobble
r_landing_quality to grade landing smoothness (may become useful once landings occur)
must_avoid: Increasing r_approach_region weight
Adding dense shaping components that overshadow the terminal goal (e.g., a large altitude-based reward that replicates hovering)
Making terminal event bonuses non-one-time (would risk repeated exploitation)
Reducing all shaping components to near zero without a reliable terminal gradient
reflection_strategy.expected_effect: Mean episode reward will likely decrease significantly as the agent shifts from hovering toward landing attempts. Progress score may initially stall, but after retraining the agent should begin to achieve the terminal event, yielding a non-zero success rate and substantially reduced unstable contact behavior.
reflection_strategy.risk_analysis: If the terminal event still cannot be triggered even after the coupled change, the total reward could collapse because the now-reduced dense shaping no longer provides a sufficient learning signal. The policy might get stuck or degrade. Rollback and a more structural search (e.g., adding a soft descent reward or a small negative altitude penalty) would be necessary. Additionally, increasing terminal bonus weight too much might cause the agent to crash repeatedly in a failed attempt to trigger the event, but the one-time nature mitigates looping.

reflection_report.reflection_summary: The agent has learned to hover near the landing pad, maximizing r_approach_region, but fails to land successfully; terminal event bonuses never trigger, indicating a persistent shaping-goal mismatch. Previous edit (decrease r_approach_region weight by 0.6, relax stable landing duration to 1) did not resolve the issue; success rate remains zero, unstable contact persists, and no terminal reward has been paid.

The terminal event condition (stable_landing_condition) is still too strict given the agent's current capabilities – it never achieves the required stability even for 1 step

reusable_lessons: A purely dense reward structure can lead to shaping-goal mismatch; terminal rewards are necessary to signal task completion
One-time event bonuses protect against exploitation if they can be triggered
failed_or_weak_lessons: The edit (decrease r_approach_region weight factor 0.6 + add_duration_condition to r_stable_landing_once steps=1) was too weak – it did not change terminal payment or landing success
Solo reduction of a dominant component without a sufficiently strong terminal boost is likely to fail
avoid_actions: Solo decrease_weight on dominant shaping component without a simultaneous significant terminal boost
Tiny adustments (e.g., <30% weight reduction) to a dominant component when terminal signal is completely absent
Increasing the weight of terminal events without verifying they can actually be triggered
recommended_actions: Significantly reduce r_approach_region weight (e.g., another 40%-60% reduction) to lower its contribution below the sum of other shaping rewards
Substantially increase the weight of r_stable_landing_once (e.g., 2× to 5×) to make it a dominant incentive once achieved
Consider adding a soft descent bonus (e.g., negative altitude reward) to bridge the gap between hovering and the terminal condition, but keep it moderate to avoid new dominance
Retrain after any further reduction to see if terminal event becomes reachable

strategy.why_atomic_or_separable: Reducing r_approach_region weight significantly could collapse total reward before the agent learns to trigger the terminal event; therefore, a simultaneous strong increase in terminal bonus weight is needed to maintain a viable learning signal. Without the coupled package, the agent may lose all dense guidance and fail to improve.
editor_constraints: Do not remove r_approach_region entirely; it provides useful early guidance to approach the pad
The total shaping reward per episode should remain within [0, 300] to prevent reward scale explosion
Terminal event bonuses must remain one-time per episode to avoid exploitation
If relaxing a duration condition further is possible, ensure it does not make the event trivially exploitable (e.g., a single frame of contact without stability)
Do not increase r_energy penalty beyond -0.05 per step to avoid discouraging main engine use
Any new shaping component (if added) must not dominate the combined terminal incentives
must_preserve: r_approach_progress (delta positive only) to maintain a directional signal toward the pad
r_stability to penalize excessive wobble
r_landing_quality to grade landing smoothness (may become useful once landings occur)
must_avoid: Increasing r_approach_region weight
Adding dense shaping components that overshadow the terminal goal (e.g., a large altitude-based reward that replicates hovering)
Making terminal event bonuses non-one-time (would risk repeated exploitation)
Reducing all shaping components to near zero without a reliable terminal gradient
strategy.expected_effect: Mean episode reward will likely decrease significantly as the agent shifts from hovering toward landing attempts. Progress score may initially stall, but after retraining the agent should begin to achieve the terminal event, yielding a non-zero success rate and substantially reduced unstable contact behavior.
strategy.risk_analysis: If the terminal event still cannot be triggered even after the coupled change, the total reward could collapse because the now-reduced dense shaping no longer provides a sufficient learning signal. The policy might get stuck or degrade. Rollback and a more structural search (e.g., adding a soft descent reward or a small negative altitude penalty) would be necessary. Additionally, increasing terminal bonus weight too much might cause the agent to crash repeatedly in a failed attempt to trigger the event, but the one-time nature mitigates looping.

package_should_be_rejected_if: Any edit reduces r_approach_region weight to zero without a clear explanation of how the agent will navigate to the pad
The terminal event bonus weight is increased beyond 5× its current value without evidence that it can be triggered at all
Multiple new dense shaping components are added, risking a return to pure shaping dominance
Total episode reward scale (mean) is pushed below 1 or above 500
package_should_be_accepted_if: It reduces r_approach_region weight by at least an additional 30% from its current value (i.e., factor ≤ 0.7 applied to current weight 0.9)
It increases the weight of r_stable_landing_once by at least 50% (e.g., weight ≥ 60)
It preserves all existing terminal one_time flags and does not remove the duration condition completely
If a new component is added, it is a simple descent indicator (e.g., negative altitude) with weight ≤ 0.5
safety_notes: Monitor contact_toggle_mean after the edit; a decrease indicates that bouncing is reduced or landing is more stable
Check that r_approach_progress still has a non-zero trigger rate, ensuring some approach signal persists
After retraining, verify that success_episode_rate becomes non-zero; if not, further relax terminal condition or add intermediate reward may be needed
Keep r_safe_contact_once as is for now; it may become useful as a stepping stone

reflection_report.reflection_summary: The agent has learned to hover near the landing pad, maximizing r_approach_region, but fails to land successfully; terminal event bonuses never trigger, indicating a persistent shaping-goal mismatch. Previous edit (decrease r_approach_region weight by 0.6, relax stable landing duration to 1) did not resolve the issue; success rate remains zero, unstable contact persists, and no terminal reward has been paid.

observed_facts: episode_length_mean=1000 (all episodes run to step limit)
previous edit only produced a small semantic score delta (+0.0177) without changing terminal reward payment or success rate
likely_true_failures: The agent does not land; task not solved
Shaping rewards (especially r_approach_region) incentivize proximity without terminal completion
The policy is stuck in a local optimum of hovering/bouncing near the pad
likely_false_positives: repeated_event_exploitation is a consequence of bouncing, not deliberate reward hacking
high_reward_low_progress flag is false; component_dominance flag is also false (raw_component_dominance false), so it is not simple dominance hacking
root_cause_hypotheses: r_approach_region weight (still 0.9 after decrease by 0.6) remains too high relative to terminal reward, making hovering locally optimal
The terminal event condition (stable_landing_condition) is still too strict given the agent's current capabilities – it never achieves the required stability even for 1 step
Lack of any intermediate reward for descending or initiating a landing attempt leaves the agent without a gradient toward the terminal event

reusable_lessons: A purely dense reward structure can lead to shaping-goal mismatch; terminal rewards are necessary to signal task completion
One-time event bonuses protect against exploitation if they can be triggered
failed_or_weak_lessons: The edit (decrease r_approach_region weight factor 0.6 + add_duration_condition to r_stable_landing_once steps=1) was too weak – it did not change terminal payment or landing success
Solo reduction of a dominant component without a sufficiently strong terminal boost is likely to fail
avoid_actions: Solo decrease_weight on dominant shaping component without a simultaneous significant terminal boost
Tiny adustments (e.g., <30% weight reduction) to a dominant component when terminal signal is completely absent
Increasing the weight of terminal events without verifying they can actually be triggered
recommended_actions: Significantly reduce r_approach_region weight (e.g., another 40%-60% reduction) to lower its contribution below the sum of other shaping rewards
Substantially increase the weight of r_stable_landing_once (e.g., 2× to 5×) to make it a dominant incentive once achieved
Consider adding a soft descent bonus (e.g., negative altitude reward) to bridge the gap between hovering and the terminal condition, but keep it moderate to avoid new dominance
Retrain after any further reduction to see if terminal event becomes reachable

lesson_assessments.reason: Previous coupled edit (decrease r_approach_region weight by 0.6, relax stable landing duration) gave only a tiny semantic gain and did not change landing success or terminal payment. The lesson is weak and its specific combination was insufficient. However, it confirms that solo shaping weight reduction without a stronger terminal boost is ineffective. We reuse the insight that a more aggressive rebalancing is needed, but not the exact edit plan.

failed_or_weak_lessons: The edit (decrease r_approach_region weight factor 0.6 + add_duration_condition to r_stable_landing_once steps=1) was too weak – it did not change terminal payment or landing success
avoid_actions: Solo decrease_weight on dominant shaping component without a simultaneous significant terminal boost
Tiny adustments (e.g., <30% weight reduction) to a dominant component when terminal signal is completely absent
Increasing the weight of terminal events without verifying they can actually be triggered
recommended_actions: Significantly reduce r_approach_region weight (e.g., another 40%-60% reduction) to lower its contribution below the sum of other shaping rewards
Substantially increase the weight of r_stable_landing_once (e.g., 2× to 5×) to make it a dominant incentive once achieved
Retrain after any further reduction to see if terminal event becomes reachable

reward_editor.rationale: Agent never lands; r_approach_region (still 0.9 after previous reduction) dominates and terminal bonus (40) never triggers. A coupled package further cuts approach weight by factor 0.6 (to ~0.54) to weaken the hovering incentive, and doubles the stable landing bonus to 80 to strongly reward landing completion once achievable. The terminal event’s one‑time flag prevents exploitation, and the duration condition (already relaxed to 1 step) remains unchanged. This rebalancing pushes the gradient toward landing without removing essential approach guidance.
reward_editor.expected_effect: Mean episode reward will drop initially as hovering reward decreases, but after retraining the agent should begin to attempt landings; success rate will eventually rise, unstable contact will decrease, and terminal reward will be paid in at least some episodes.
reward_editor.risk_analysis: If the terminal event still cannot be triggered even with doubled bonus, total reward could collapse because reduced dense shaping no longer provides enough learning signal. In that case, further structural search (e.g., adding a moderate descent-based reward) would be needed. The risk is acceptable because the shaping still provides guidance (r_approach_progress, r_stability, r_landing_quality) and the bonus weight increase is within safe bounds (max 5×).

what_failed: Previous coupled package (decrease r_approach_region by 0.6 + relax stable landing duration) did not resolve zero terminal payment
avoid_next: Solo decrease_weight on shaping without terminal boost
Applying the exact same combination again
recommend_next: After this edit, if success remains zero, consider adding a descent/altitude reward to bridge to the terminal condition
distilled_lessons.applicability_notes: When terminal event is never paid, strengthen the terminal incentive alongside reducing dominant dense reward; duration relaxation alone was insufficient.
